name: Independence Power Holdings, Inc.
business: Independence Power Holdings, Inc. is an energy technology company that provides software-enabled control and management of battery energy storage systems (BESS) deployed at unconventional oil and gas facilities. Its primary business is the software and hardware development, implementation, and installation of industrial battery management and monitoring systems for high voltage battery storage systems. The company sells software licenses, subscription fees, and related services to oil and gas operators and rural electric cooperatives.

exposures.sentence: The Company s primary business activity is the software and hardware development, implementation and installation of industrial battery management and monitoring systems for high voltage battery storage systems.

The Company s battery energy storage system ( BESS ) solution is targeted to be deployed at unconventional oil and gas operations, initially in the Permian Basin in western Texas and southeastern New Mexico and other key energy-producing regions in the United States, and ultimately around the world.

catalysts.event: Completion of forward stock split of Class A Common Stock and Class B Common Stock
catalysts.sentence: Effective February 7, 2026, the Company completed the Forward Split of its Class A Common Stock and Class B Common Stock.

catalysts.event: Entry into line of credit agreement with Independence Investors for up to $4 million
catalysts.sentence: Effective March 1, 2026, the Company entered into a line of credit agreement with Independence Investors, its majority shareholder, that allows for advances up to $4 million at 4% interest, with all outstanding principal and unpaid interest due on April 30, 2027.

catalysts.event: Receipt of scheduled cash interest payment of approximately $1.7 million under the GridCore Note
catalysts.sentence: In March 2026, the Company received a scheduled cash interest payment of approximately $1.7 million under the GridCore Note.

catalysts.event: Entry into Administrative Services Agreement with Rincon II LLC
catalysts.sentence: Effective March 27, 2026, the Company entered into an Administrative Services Agreement (the ASA Agreement ) with Rincon II LLC ( Rincon ), a related party, whereby Rincon will provide administrative support services to the Company at $30,000 per month plus out-of-pocket expenses.

catalysts.event: Full commercial deployment of software platform across BESS Fleet by year-end
catalysts.sentence: The Company will continue development, testing, and commercial deployment of its proprietary software platform across the BESS Fleet throughout 2026, with the objective of full commercial deployment across the fleet by year-end.

tamClaims.claim: Electricity demand in the Permian Basin is projected to increase from 3.4 GW in 2022 to over 11.9 GW by 2032
tamClaims.sentence: According to ERCOT load growth projections, electricity demand in the Permian Basin is expected to increase from approximately 3.4 gigawatts in 2022 to potentially more than 11.9 gigawatts by 2032, reflecting more than 300 percent growth over the decade.

tamClaims.claim: Total basin-wide load could reach 26 GW by 2038
tamClaims.sentence: ERCOT further forecasts that total basin-wide load including upstream, midstream, and ancillary industrial activities could reach 26 gigawatts by 2038.

tamClaims.claim: Existing regional electricity shortfall estimated at 8–13 GW
tamClaims.sentence: Hart Energy reported in 2024 an existing regional electricity shortfall estimated at between 8 and 13 gigawatts, depending on methodology.

tamClaims.claim: Average drilling rig consumes approximately 50 barrels of diesel per day, with annual fuel costs of $1.83–$2.29 million per rig
tamClaims.sentence: RBN Energy estimates that the average drilling rig in the Permian Basin consumes approximately 50 barrels of diesel per day, equivalent to over 750,000 gallons per year.
